title: Operators on Visual Selections
subtitle: Any operator works on a Visual range. Plus a few visual-only ones.
summary: After selecting visually, any operator ({key:d}, {key:c}, {key:y}, {key:g}{key:U}, etc.) acts on the selection. A few operators are visual-only: {key:u}/{key:U} for case, {key:J} for join, {key:>} / {key:<} for indent.

blocks.text: After Visual selection, every operator key acts on the selection — no motion needed. The selection *is* the range.

blocks.title: Visual-Only Single-Key Operators
blocks.text:
In *normal mode*, lowercase {key:u} is *undo*. In *visual mode*, {key:u} is *lowercase the selection* — a single-key shortcut for {key:g}{key:u}. Similarly:

  • {key:u} = lowercase
  • {key:U} = uppercase
  • {key:~} = toggle case

These exist because the {key:g} prefix is awkward when you've already done the work of selecting. The same convenience: {key:>} and {key:<} act *once* in Visual without needing a motion (they need {key:>}{key:>} to act on a line in *normal mode*).
After indenting a Visual selection with {key:>}, the selection is lost — but {key:g}{key:v} brings it right back so you can {key:>} again.
